title: Code Comments
description: Leave notes for yourself and other developers.

slides.title: Invisible Notes 📝
slides.content:
## What are comments?

Code Comments are text notes added to your code. 

The important part is: **the computer completely ignores them**! They are invisible to the machine, making them perfect for leaving explanations for humans (or your future self).

slides.title: Single-Line Comments ➖
slides.content:
To create a comment on a single line, use two forward slashes: `//`.

Anything written after `//` on that line will be ignored.

quizData.question: Which symbol is used for a single-line comment in Kotlin?
quizData.explanation: Two forward slashes `//` start a single-line comment.

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments 🔲
slides.content:
If you have a very long explanation, you can use a multi-line comment.

It starts with `/*` and ends exactly when you close it with `*/`.
slides.codeSnippet:
/* 
  This is a very long comment.
  It spans across multiple lines.
  It's great for detailed documentation.
*/
println("Back to normal code!")

quizData.question: How do you close a multi-line comment?
quizData.explanation: Multi-line comments open with `/*` and close with `*/`.
